title: centaurea phrygia subsp. ratezatensis
intro: Centaurea phrygia subsp. ratezatensis, also known as Ratezat cornflower, is a plant species in the daisy family that is native to Romania. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and soothing respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of Ratezat cornflower include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
